description: Edge-first self-hosted control plane for apps, fleet, GitOps, drift, releases, security, telemetry, and disaster recovery.

sections.content:
# Pocket Lab Architecture Context

Pocket Lab is an edge-first self-hosted control plane for app deployment, GitOps, fleet management, drift detection, security posture, NOC telemetry, release orchestration, and disaster recovery.

It is designed around a modern control-plane pattern:

```text
UI → FastAPI → NATS / JetStream → Worker → Events → UI
```

The system intentionally avoids legacy shell-command compatibility paths for control-plane writes. Typed operations, NATS subjects, generated API contracts, and event-sourced workflow state form the main architectural backbone.

![](embed:system-context)

# Container Architecture

Pocket Lab's primary containers are:

- React / Vite PWA
- FastAPI Control API
- NATS / JetStream
- Pocket Lab Worker
- Event Journal / Workflow Store
- Typed Operations Catalog
- MkDocs Documentation Portal
- Domain services for Catalog, GitOps, Drift, Fleet, Vault, Security, Telemetry, Release, and Disaster Recovery

![](embed:container-view)

# Event Driven Runtime

The runtime uses NATS and JetStream as the durable command and event backbone.

FastAPI publishes typed commands, workers consume them, and events are emitted for:

- UI state
- operation logs
- workflow recovery
- retry handling
- auditability
- dead-letter analysis

![](embed:event-driven-runtime)

![](embed:typed-operation-flow)

# Deployment Architecture

Pocket Lab has two primary deployment contexts:

1. Android / Termux edge device.
2. Ubuntu developer workstation.

The Android / Termux target is the key production-like edge deployment path. The Ubuntu dev environment supports deterministic validation, test gates, and release dry-runs.

![](embed:android-termux-deployment)

![](embed:developer-workstation-deployment)

# Enterprise Security Review Views

Tier 5B adds dedicated Structurizr views for security and operations review.

These views make the most sensitive runtime boundaries explicit:

- Security, policy, Vault/OpenBao, and OPA relationships.
- NATS command and event boundaries.
- Audit evidence and DLQ paths.

## Security / Policy / Vault / OPA Relationships

![](embed:security-review)

## NATS Command and Event Boundaries

![](embed:nats-command-event-boundaries)

## Audit and DLQ Paths

![](embed:audit-and-dlq-paths)

# Operations Governance

Pocket Lab governance is centered on typed operations.

Every important UI write action should map to:

- a typed operation
- a FastAPI endpoint
- a NATS command subject
- success and failure events
- audit records where applicable
- generated documentation

The generated operations catalog under `operations/*.yaml` acts as the Backstage-style source of truth for operation metadata.

Routes retry-exhausted messages to dead-letter subjects

Writes auditable operation, policy, vault, release, and recovery evidence
Publishes failure outcomes when commands cannot be recovered
Preserves failed command/event evidence for audit and recovery

people.description: Uses Pocket Lab to install apps, manage devices, monitor health, run GitOps, rotate secrets, and apply releases.
people.name: Operator / Home Lab Admin

containers.description: Operator UI with Professional and Simple Experience modes.

relationships.description: Calls REST API and WebSocket event stream

containers.description: HTTP API, WebSocket event stream, OpenAPI contract, typed operation submission, release endpoints, health endpoints.
containers.name: FastAPI Control API

relationships.description: Streams operation events and live status updates
Reads operation status, events, and workflow state
Reads audit evidence for reviews and operator status
Refreshes catalog and starts deploy workflows
Requests policy decisions before sensitive control-plane actions
Coordinates secret operations through the vault adapter

containers.description: Durable command, event, audit, telemetry, and DLQ streams.
containers.name: NATS / JetStream

relationships.description: Delivers durable commands to the worker
Routes retry-exhausted messages to dead-letter subjects

containers.description: Consumes typed commands, executes domain handlers, emits lifecycle events, writes audit records.
containers.name: Pocket Lab Worker

relationships.description: Records operation lifecycle and workflow state
Writes auditable operation, policy, vault, release, and recovery evidence
Executes security scans and OPA configuration
Publishes failure outcomes when commands cannot be recovered
Executes catalog refresh and blueprint deployment
Uses secret leases during execution when required

containers.description: Stores operation lifecycle, recovery state, workflow snapshots, and event-sourced workflow history.
containers.name: Event Journal / Workflow Store

containers.description: Stores auditable security, release, vault, policy, and DLQ evidence for review and recovery.
containers.name: Audit Trail / Evidence Store

containers.description: Represents JetStream retry exhaustion and dead-letter subjects for failed commands and events.
containers.name: DLQ / Retry Subjects

relationships.description: Preserves failed command/event evidence for audit and recovery

containers.description: Backstage-style operation metadata source and generated typed operation catalog.
containers.name: Typed Operations Catalog

containers.description: Generated documentation for OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, Typed Operations, Structurizr, runbooks, architecture, and readiness.
containers.name: MkDocs Documentation Portal

containers.description: Pulls and validates desired state using typed operations and Git abstraction.
containers.name: GitOps / Source Adapter

relationships.description: Reads desired state and release source

containers.description: App catalog refresh, blueprint deploy, package metadata, app source modes.
containers.name: Blueprint / App Catalog Engine

containers.description: Scans desired vs actual state and manages preview, approve, apply, ignore workflows.
containers.name: Drift Engine

containers.description: Fleet join, node health, telemetry, heartbeat, and mesh onboarding workflows.
containers.name: Fleet Agent Control

relationships.description: Uses mesh connectivity and join material

containers.description: Secret rotation and dynamic secret lease workflows.
containers.name: Identity Vault Adapter

containers.description: Security scans, OPA configuration, policy checks, secret redaction validation.
containers.name: Security / Policy Guardrails

containers.description: CPU, memory, disk, health, worker, NATS, and live status telemetry.
containers.name: NOC Telemetry Adapter

containers.description: Self-update check/apply workflow with backup, sync, deploy, verify, and catalog refresh stages.
containers.name: Release Workflow Orchestrator

containers.description: Backup, verify, restore preview, restore execution, manifest validation.
containers.name: Backup / Disaster Recovery Engine
